bead.title: openrouter health gate: add filter reasons and provider-eligibility plumbing in routing engine
bead.description: Foundation for parent fizeau-0bc93861. Adds the four new FilterReason constants the openrouter health gate needs (credential_missing, credential_invalid, credit_exhausted, provider_unreachable) and plumbs a routing.Inputs map keyed by provider name into the engine candidate loop, so any provider can be marked eligible:false with a typed FilterReason and freshness timestamp without baking openrouter specifics into the engine. No openrouter wiring yet — that lands in the credential-check and credit-probe children.
bead.acceptance:
1. internal/routing/engine.go declares FilterReasonCredentialMissing, FilterReasonCredentialInvalid, FilterReasonCreditExhausted, FilterReasonProviderUnreachable as FilterReason values with the documented snake_case string forms ('credential_missing','credential_invalid','credit_exhausted','provider_unreachable').
2. routing.Inputs has a new map field (e.g. ProviderEligibilityOverrides map[string]ProviderEligibilityOverride{FilterReason, ProbeAt}) that the engine consults inside buildHarnessCandidates after the existing ProbeUnreachable gate. When a provider key matches, the candidate is set eligible=false with the typed FilterReason from the map and Reason populated with a stable human-readable string.
3. An explicit provider/harness/model pin bypasses the override exactly like the existing endpoint_unreachable gate.
4. Engine-level unit test in internal/routing/ (e.g. credential_health_gate_test.go) asserts:
   a. Each of the four new FilterReasons gates a matching provider when no pin is present.
   b. Explicit provider pin bypasses each gate.
   c. Map is nil-safe and absent providers are unaffected.
5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... green and full go test ./... green.
6. No regression in service_adr005_test.go or service_routing_test.go.
